title: dianthus crinitus subsp. tetralepis
intro: Dianthus crinitus subsp. tetralepis, also known as the fringed pink or fragrant pink, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Dianthus crinitus subsp. tetralepis include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making it a useful treatment for minor cuts and scrapes. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote healing and prevent infection.
